title: Wheelbarrow
body:
![An infographic showcasing 4 oxidation variants of the wheelbarrow, how to pick up a mob (by riding into it) and ejecting it (by pressing "Z")](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/F0jGC2As/images/9822c32ee7e7ef80f525f6b3c7009dce6b45ceba.jpeg)

_Have you ever been annoyed trying to gently shove a villager or an animal into a boat/minecart to then either have to put down and destroy rails over hundreds of blocks, or only being able to go by water?_

# Introducing the wheelbarrow! While riding it you can pick up any mob (that's small enough to fit) by riding into it. Then transport it wherever you desire over any terrain

![A gif of the copper wheelbarrow alternating through 4 oxidation variants with a villager inside and a wooden wheel spinning](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/F0jGC2As/images/cf10b51c88fa0cec6b464435f7783c47ab9f8feb.gif)

## It's made of copper and comes in 4 default oxidation variants

![Minecraft crafting table inventory with the wheelbarrow recipe and 4 oxidation level item variants](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/36ef6285ae8053499ea4cb2ec87dcf43a8ecbdfa.png)

### Transported mobs can be ~~ejected~~ yeeted with a keypress (Z by default)

![A gif of ejecting multiple villagers from the wheelbarrow in the nether into lava](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/F0jGC2As/images/88e73eb74d2800f56668b3440201268690e2ad94.gif)

### You can also configure the number of mobs that can be transported at a time (default: 1)

![4 wheelbarrows next to each other each featuring a different set of mobs stacked on top of one another](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/F0jGC2As/images/c38d35afa9cb6493bc55872015be94edc3319bed.jpeg)
